name: Aýna Circular Route
description: Cycle the moderate 14.5-mile Aýna circular route, gaining 1465 feet with scenic viewpoints and varied terrain in Sierra del Segura.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Ayna traverse a mountainous landscape characterized by hills, valleys, and the winding Mundo River. The region features extensive pine and holm oak forests, providing a lush backdrop for cycling. Routes often include significant elevation changes and turns, offering varied terrain for touring cyclists. Ayna's proximity to the Calares del Mundo y de la Sima Natural Park further enhances its appeal for outdoor exploration. Best touring cycling routes around Ayna The most popular touring cycling route is Los Infiernos Viewpoint – Aýna loop from Royo Odrea, a 42.3 miles (68.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 39 minutes to complete, featuring challenging climbs and panoramic views of the valley. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Mirador La Rodea Grande – Los Infiernos Viewpoint loop from Alcadozo, a difficult 41.4 miles (66.6 km) path. This route winds through mountainous landscapes, offering vistas from multiple viewpoints. Local touring cyclists also love the Aýna Circular Route, a 14.5 miles (23.3 km) trail leading through valleys and hills, often completed in about 1 hour 48 minutes. Touring cycling around Ayna is defined by mountainous terrain, pine and holm oak forests, and the Mundo River. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Ayna are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Ayna's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Ayna?
A: Touring cycling routes around Ayna are characterized by a mountainous landscape with significant elevation changes, hills, and valleys. You'll encounter winding roads and scenic paths through extensive pine and holm oak forests, often alongside the Mundo River. The region offers varied terrain, primarily featuring moderate to difficult routes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Ayna?
A: Yes, Ayna offers circular touring cycling routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1068189706/ayna-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aýna Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 14.5-mile (23.3 km) trail that leads through valleys and hills, typically completed in about 1 hour 48 minutes.

Q: What natural features can I see while cycling in the Ayna region?
A: The Ayna region is rich in natural beauty. You can expect to see the picturesque Mundo River, extensive pine and holm oak forests, and the dramatic landscapes of the Calares del Mundo y de la Sima Natural Park. A notable natural attraction is the spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5318691" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batán Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; on the Bogarra river, which is approximately 10 meters high and surrounded by dense forests.

Q: Are there any routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Ayna provide stunning panoramic views due to the mountainous terrain. For instance, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20129941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador La Rodea Grande – Los Infiernos Viewpoint loop from Alcadozo&lt;/a&gt; winds through mountainous landscapes, offering vistas from multiple viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5812222" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Infiernos Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; itself provides incredible views of the valley and the Mundo River.

Q: What is the difficulty level of touring cycling routes around Ayna?
A: The touring cycling routes around Ayna are generally challenging. Out of 22 recorded routes, 4 are classified as moderate and 18 as difficult. This indicates that most routes involve significant climbs and descents, making them suitable for cyclists looking for a rewarding physical challenge.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks along the cycling routes?
A: While cycling, you'll be in a region with a rich history. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5596072" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aýna&lt;/a&gt; itself dates back to Roman times and features the ruins of Yedra Castle, an 11th-century Islamic fortress. Some routes may pass through or near these historically significant areas, offering a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about cycling in Ayna?
A: The touring cycling routes in Ayna are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 3 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging climbs, panoramic views, and the diverse natural features like the Mundo River and extensive forests that make for a rewarding experience.

Q: Can I find a long-distance touring cycling route that explores the natural park?
A: Yes, you can explore the natural park on a longer route. The difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1363404784/embalse-de-la-fuensanta-y-rio-tus-desde-molinicos-circular-por-el-parque-natural-de-los-calares-del-mundo-y-de-la-sima" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de la Fuensanta y río Tús desde Molinicos — circular por el Parque Natural de Los Calares del Mundo y de la Sima&lt;/a&gt; is a 32.7-mile (52.7 km) circular route with over 1000 meters of elevation gain, offering an immersive experience within the Calares del Mundo y de la Sima Natural Park.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Ayna?
A: Spain, including the Ayna region, is celebrated for its diverse cycling opportunities and excellent climate, making it an ideal destination for cycle touring throughout the year. However, given the mountainous terrain and potential for significant elevation changes, spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for challenging rides, avoiding the peak heat of summer.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through the town of Bogarra?
A: Yes, there are routes that incorporate the town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6006591" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bogarra&lt;/a&gt;. For example, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24528429" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of Bogarra – Bogarra loop from Bogarra&lt;/a&gt; is a 23.9-mile (38.5 km) route that starts and ends in Bogarra, featuring over 1000 meters of elevation gain.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in the Ayna area?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes available around Ayna, offering a variety of options for cyclists. These routes range in length and difficulty, primarily catering to moderate to difficult skill levels.
tips: The viewpoint also called "Sidecar" for the replica of the scooter from the film "Amanece que no es poco" by José Luis Cuerda. The film was shot between the towns of Aýna, Molinicos and Letur in 1988. The viewpoint offers wonderful views of the rocks on which the town of Aýna is located. Very recommendable.
A nice and quiet gravel road between the towns of La Navazuela and Villarejo to avoid the road. It is a path among the pines, enjoy its smell.

A balcony-viewpoint located at a higher level than the road (you have to go up a staircase), overlooking the valley on a large rock protected by a stone wall and a metal fence. It has an information panel. Spectacular views of Aýna and the mountains.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Aýna — circular route for touring cyclists?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate, but it's part of an area known for demanding road rides with significant elevation gains. While some sections are well-paved, be prepared for potential unpaved segments that might require pushing your bike, especially if you're not accustomed to routes demanding very good fitness.

faqs.question: Where does the Aýna — circular route begin and end?
faqs.answer: As a circular route, it forms a loop, meaning you will finish where you started. The exact starting point can be flexible, but it typically begins and ends within the vicinity of Aýna village, allowing for convenient access to amenities.

faqs.question: Are there parking facilities available for cyclists near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, parking is generally available in and around the village of Aýna, which serves as a common starting point for this circular route. Look for designated parking areas for tourists or along village streets where permitted.

faqs.question: What scenic highlights and viewpoints can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers unusual nature and picturesque villages. You'll encounter stunning vistas from viewpoints such as Mirador La Rodea Grande and the Devil's Viewpoint. The area is also famous for the 'Los Infiernos Viewpoint' (Sidecar), offering wonderful views of the rocks on which Aýna is located.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly crosses sections of the Sendero del Mundo, Camino Cuesta del Santo, and Camino Viejo. It also touches upon the GR-67 Sendero del Mundo.

faqs.question: Is the Aýna — circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on many trails in the Sierra del Segura region, it's important to keep them on a leash, especially given the presence of wildlife and potential for livestock. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically during the spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) months. During these periods, the weather is generally milder and more pleasant for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for cycling in the Sierra del Segura area?
faqs.answer: Generally, no specific permit or entrance fee is required for cycling on public trails like this one within the Sierra del Segura region. However, always respect local regulations, especially if you venture into protected natural areas like the Calares del Mundo y de la Sima Natural Park, where specific rules might apply.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options for cyclists near Aýna?
faqs.answer: Aýna is a picturesque village and offers various amenities for visitors. You can find cafes, restaurants, and rural accommodation options that cater to tourists and cyclists. It's advisable to check availability, especially during peak seasons.

faqs.question: Is it possible to rent a bike in or near Aýna for this route?
faqs.answer: While Aýna itself is a smaller village, there might be limited local options for bike rental. For a wider selection, it's often more reliable to arrange rentals in larger nearby towns or cities before heading to Aýna, especially if you require specific touring bicycle models.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty rating, significant elevation gain, and potential for unpaved sections, this specific Aýna — circular route may be too challenging for younger children or families seeking an easy ride. There might be easier, shorter routes in the broader Aýna area that are more suitable for family cycling.
